highlights.text: I wrote last Monday at 4pm (when we were 6340s): “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.

Rallies like this don’t come for free though and as I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “After a move like this, ES often needs to digest the move…My general lean if I had to give one though is that ES can cooloff now and do some backtesting and work on structure.” We put in the first real dip since July 15th yesterday and it ultimately continued today.
My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
This gives us a chance to long and as readers know, I do that via one trade: The Failed Breakdown.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a textbook Failed Breakdown we had last Thursday morning where ES lost and recovered a significant low at 6395, giving us the squeeze to this weeks highs.
This failed breakdown was interesting because it confluenced with a bull flag.

post_title: FOMC Tomorrow. Expect Volatility In SPX, Can Bulls Keep The Run Going? July 30th Plan
